name: Cold Snap — Northeast 10 Days
description: Sustained Arctic air mass moves into the Northeast for 10 days, driving residential and commercial heating demand well above seasonal norms.

name: LNG Export Disruption — Gulf Coast 14 Days
description: A major Gulf Coast LNG terminal goes offline for maintenance or an unplanned outage, reducing export capacity significantly for two weeks.

name: Appalachian Production Freeze-Off
description: Extreme cold in key Appalachian producing regions causes wellhead freeze-offs, reducing dry gas production for roughly one week.

name: Hurricane — Gulf Coast Production Disruption
description: A major hurricane tracks through the Gulf of Mexico, forcing offshore platform evacuations and briefly disrupting pipeline infrastructure.

name: Geopolitical Shock — European LNG Demand Spike
description: A synthetic supply disruption in European pipeline infrastructure triggers a surge in spot LNG demand from European buyers, pulling more US LNG exports.

name: Heat Wave — National Cooling Demand Surge
description: An early-season or late-season heat wave across multiple major population centers elevates power-sector gas burn beyond seasonal norms.

name: OPEC+ Surprise Cut — 1.5 Mb/d
description: OPEC+ announces an unscheduled production cut of 1.5 million barrels per day to defend prices, tightening global balances over the following quarter.

name: Strait of Hormuz Disruption
description: An escalation in the Gulf threatens transit through the Strait of Hormuz, removing barrels from the seaborne market and adding a geopolitical risk premium.

name: China Demand Slowdown
description: A sharper-than-expected slowdown in Chinese industrial activity and travel cuts apparent oil demand, loosening balances into the back half of the year.

name: Coordinated SPR Release
description: IEA member states announce a coordinated strategic petroleum reserve release, adding government barrels to the market to cap prices over the next month.
